uc7_decomposition.name: API Endpoint & Database Schema
uc7_decomposition.description: Create /api/sms/send-manual/route.ts endpoint with lead permission validation and message persistence
success_criteria: POST /api/sms/send-manual accepts lead_id, message_body, ai_assist boolean
Endpoint validates agent has permission for lead (via lead_agent_id)
Message stored in Supabase messages table with all metadata
Returns {success: true, message_id, status} on success
Returns {success: false, error} with proper error codes on failure
TypeScript strict mode: zero errors
Unit tests: 5/5 passing
uc7_decomposition.technical_notes: Build on existing sendSms() in lib/twilio.ts. No new dependencies. Use existing lead permission pattern from UC-6.

uc7_decomposition.name: Message Thread UI Component
uc7_decomposition.description: Build MessageThread component to display conversation history with proper styling and timestamps
success_criteria: Displays chronological message history (inbound gray, outbound blue, AI purple)
Shows timestamp + delivery status (sent/delivered/failed) for each message
Mobile-responsive layout (tested on 375px width)
Handles empty state (no messages) gracefully
Auto-scrolls to latest message on mount
Component renders without errors in React StrictMode
uc7_decomposition.technical_notes: Use existing dashboard styling pattern from lead detail page. Consider virtual scrolling for 100+ messages later.
dependencies: Lead detail page exists (verified in DASHBOARD.md)

uc7_decomposition.name: SMS Composer & AI Assist
uc7_decomposition.description: Build SmsComposer with input field, AI Assist button (generates contextual suggestions), and Send button with validation
success_criteria: Text input allows up to 160 characters (single SMS)
Character counter shows remaining characters
AI Assist button triggers AI endpoint with conversation context
Suggested response appears in modal/popover for agent approval
Agent can edit suggestion before confirming
Send button disabled until message > 0 characters
Opt-out compliance check: verify lead.consent_sms before sending
UX: loading state during AI generation (spinner, disabled button)
uc7_decomposition.technical_notes: AI Assist calls /api/sms/ai-suggest endpoint (new). Feed last 5 messages + lead profile. Use Claude for suggestions.
blockers_or_gaps: AI model selection: Use Claude Haiku (fast, low cost) for suggestions

uc7_decomposition.name: Integration & E2E Testing
uc7_decomposition.description: Wire components into lead detail page, test full flow, verify Twilio delivery, build test suite
success_criteria: Lead detail page shows MessageThread + SmsComposer side-by-side
E2E flow: agent types message → clicks Send → message appears in thread with 'sent' status
Delivery status updates in real-time (or within 5s via polling)
Opt-out check prevents sending to opted_out leads (shows error message)
Manual test with real Twilio number receives SMS
All TypeScript errors resolved
Lighthouse performance: >85 on lead detail page
uc7_decomposition.technical_notes: Use existing polling pattern or WebSocket for real-time updates. Can start with polling (simpler), add WS later if needed.

uc8_decomposition.name: Sequence Schema & Data Model
uc8_decomposition.description: Create lead_sequences table, define sequence types, triggers, and state machine in code
success_criteria: Supabase migration creates lead_sequences table with schema: {lead_id, sequence_type, step, last_sent_at, next_send_at, status, created_at, updated_at}
Sequence types enum: 'no_response' | 'post_viewing' | 'no_show' | 'nurture'
Sequence templates defined in lib/sequences.ts with message bodies
State machine allows transitions: active → paused → completed, never reverse
Migration is idempotent (can run multiple times safely)
Database indexes on (lead_id, sequence_type) and (next_send_at) for efficient cron queries
uc8_decomposition.technical_notes: Use Supabase migrations pattern from existing schema. No external APIs needed for this milestone.

uc8_decomposition.name: Cron Job Handler & Trigger Logic
uc8_decomposition.description: Build /api/cron/follow-up/route.ts that runs hourly, identifies leads needing follow-up, and queues SMS
success_criteria: Cron handler accepts GET requests (Vercel cron trigger)
Queries leads with conditions: no_response (24h no message), post_viewing (4h after booking), no_show (30m after missed booking), nurture (7 days no activity)
Respects opt-outs: skips leads with lead.consent_sms = false or lead.dnc = true
Respects quiet hours: does not send between 9pm-12am lead timezone
Fetches conversation history for each lead to pass to AI
Calls AI for each lead to generate contextual follow-up message
For each SMS: calls /api/sms/send-manual internally (reuses UC-7 endpoint)
Updates lead_sequences: sets last_sent_at, calculates next_send_at (for next trigger delay)
Logs results: {processed: N, sent: N, skipped: N, errors: N}
Includes error handling: if AI fails, log and retry next hour (don't crash cron)
uc8_decomposition.technical_notes: Use Vercel Cron (free on Pro plan). Cron should run ~5-10 seconds per 100 leads (batch operations). Can fetch data in batches of 50.
dependencies: UC7 M1 (send-manual endpoint works, can call it from cron)
blockers_or_gaps: Timezone handling: needs lead timezone from profile (may need to add field to database or assume UTC initially)

uc8_decomposition.name: Sequence Controls & State Management
uc8_decomposition.description: Implement pause/resume logic, max 3 messages per sequence, reset on lead response
success_criteria: Pause mechanism: if lead responds to follow-up, set sequence.status = 'paused' (no more messages until manual reset)
Max messages: after 3 SMS sent for sequence_type, set status = 'completed'
Response detection: check if last_message_at > last_sent_at (indicates lead replied)
Manual pause/resume: agents can pause sequences from dashboard (future UI, logic in place)
Cron respects all state: only sends if status = 'active' AND step < 3
Logging: every state transition logged with timestamp
No race conditions: database constraints prevent concurrent updates
uc8_decomposition.technical_notes: Use database constraints and atomic transactions to prevent race conditions. Simple state machine, no external libraries needed.

uc8_decomposition.name: Testing & Deployment
uc8_decomposition.description: Build test suite, manual testing with fake leads, verify cron runs hourly, deploy to Vercel
success_criteria: Unit tests: sequence trigger logic (no_response, post_viewing, etc.) - 8+ tests passing
Integration test: create test lead, manually trigger cron, verify SMS sent and sequence state updated
Cron schedule verified: vercel.json has correct schedule (0 * * * * = top of every hour)
Manual cron test: call /api/cron/follow-up?test=true, observe logs, verify no side effects
Quiet hours respected: test does not send during 9pm-12am
Opt-out respected: test does not send to opted_out leads
No TypeScript errors
Build succeeds on Vercel (deploy preview)
Cron runs successfully at least once (observe in Vercel cron logs)
uc8_decomposition.technical_notes: Use ?test=true query param to run cron without actually sending SMS (dry-run mode for testing).

execution_plan.rationale: UC-7 must complete before UC-8 cron can call send-manual endpoint. However, UC-7-M1/M2/M3 can happen in parallel; only M4 needs to wait. UC-8-M1 can start immediately in parallel with UC-7.

phase_2_sequential.start_condition: Phase 1 complete AND UC7-M1 endpoint verified working
phase_2_sequential.completion_gate: UC7-M3 SmsComposer integrated, UC8-M2 cron logic done

phase_3_final.completion_gate: All E2E tests pass, both UCs ready for pilot

sprint_schedule.ideal: Single ~26h sprint (2-3 days continuous, or 4-5 days at 6h/day)
sprint_schedule.checkpoint_1: After Phase 1: UC-7 API works, UC-8 schema valid
sprint_schedule.checkpoint_2: After Phase 2: UC-7 UI components wired, UC-8 cron logic complete
sprint_schedule.checkpoint_3: After Phase 3: Full E2E tests pass, ready to merge to main

resource_gaps.impact: UC7-M3 requires this endpoint to generate AI suggestions
resource_gaps.resolution: Create app/api/sms/ai-suggest/route.ts. Call Claude Haiku with conversation context + lead profile. Return {suggestion: string, confidence: float}. Low priority for AI model (Haiku is sufficient).

resource_gaps.impact: UC8-M2 quiet hours feature requires lead timezone for accurate 9pm-12am check
resource_gaps.resolution: If not available: assume UTC for MVP, add field later. Can hardcode 9pm EST for testing.

resource_gaps.impact: UC-8 requires Vercel Cron which is only available on Pro plan
resource_gaps.resolution: Confirm account has Vercel Pro. If not, upgrade or implement alternative cron (e.g., external cron service like cron-job.org). Estimated cost: $20/month for Vercel Pro.

resource_gaps.impact: UC7-M4 E2E testing requires ability to receive test SMS
resource_gaps.resolution: Use existing Twilio dev number or create test number. Cost: $1/month for test number.

parallel_opportunities: UC7-M1/M2/M3 in parallel (but M4 needs all 3)
UC8-M1 in parallel with UC7-M1/M2/M3 (can start immediately)

blocking_milestones.UC8-M2: Blocks UC8-M3 (state controls depend on cron logic)
blocking_milestones.UC8-M3: Blocks UC8-M4 (testing needs full state machine)

success_metrics.uc7_success: Agent can view lead message history, type custom SMS, click 'AI Assist' for suggestions, send message, and see delivery status in real-time
success_metrics.uc8_success: Cron runs hourly, identifies leads needing follow-up (24h no response, 4h post-booking, etc.), generates contextual AI messages, sends via Twilio, updates sequence state, respects opt-outs and quiet hours
success_metrics.combined_success: Both UCs deployed to production, fully tested, zero TypeScript errors, ready for pilot recruitment

known_risks.mitigation: Use Claude Haiku (fast), set 5s timeout, show spinner to user

known_risks.risk: Race condition in sequence state updates
known_risks.mitigation: Use database constraints + atomic transactions, test concurrency
Comprehensive logging, monitor Vercel cron logs, add alerts if needed
Start with UTC/EST assumption, add field later

spawn_recommendation.suggested_prompt: You are the Dev Agent for LeadFlow AI (business-opportunities-2026 project). UC-6 is complete. UC-7 (Dashboard Manual SMS) and UC-8 (Follow-up Sequences) are now queued. I've decomposed both into milestones with clear success criteria. Execute in this order: Phase 1 (4h parallel): UC7-M1 API + UC7-M2 MessageThread + UC8-M1 Schema. Phase 2 (3h): UC7-M3 SmsComposer + UC8-M2 Cron. Phase 3 (3h): UC7-M4 Integration + UC8-M3 Controls + UC8-M4 Testing. Checkpoint after each phase. Goal: 26 hours total, both UCs production-ready for pilot launch.
spawn_recommendation.success_definition: All milestones complete with green checkmarks, E2E tests pass, zero TypeScript errors, code merged to main and deployed to Vercel staging.
spawn_recommendation.escalation_path: If blocked for >30 min, report blocker to orchestrator. If unclear on requirement, ask for clarification before proceeding.

code: Test suites (UC7 E2E, UC8 cron tests)
deployment: Vercel deployment preview tested and approved
Cron verified running hourly (check Vercel logs)
E2E test with real Twilio number
